revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:皇祖神]]
'''Kōsojin''' (皇祖神) refers to the [[Kami|Deities]] considered ancestors of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. == Overview == In modern times, this term refers to {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, a deity considered to be the personification of the {{ill|Sun|en|Sun|qid=Q525|lt=Sun|1=Sun}} according to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. The opening of the "Kamiyo" section of the *Nihon Shoki* records {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} as the "Kōso" (皇祖). The original protagonist of the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|qid=Q1084733|lt=Tenson kōrin|1=Tenson kōrin}} myth was Takamimusubi. The initial version of this myth is said to have been a straightforward story where Takamimusubi sent {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|lt=Hono Ninigi|ja|ホノニニギ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} to descend. Later additions such as Amaterasu, Oshihomimi, the five deities, sacred treasures, and divine decrees transformed the myth into its current form. Takamimusubi is also worshipped as the main deity of the {{ill|Hall of Eight Deities|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hall of Eight Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}, acting as a guardian deity of the Emperor, and is enshrined in rituals like the {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}}, and {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}. It is widely accepted that Takamimusubi was originally the ancestral deity of the Imperial House. The *Nihon Shoki* also mentions the establishment of a "Hi Matsuribe" in the court during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|qid=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}, predating the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|qid=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}}. This was an early system of worship dedicated to the sun deity. Over time, with influences from the Korean Peninsula and concepts such as the "rule of the child of the sun" seen in songs like "Homuda no Hi no Miko, Ōsazume," from the era of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, the sun deity came to be revered as the ancestral deity of the Imperial family. == Gender == Amaterasu-Ōmikami is generally regarded as a goddess, but there are also theories identifying her as a male deity. According to myth, she did not engage in traditional marriage but adopted five sons born from a ritual exchange with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. She also entrusted the {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} she bore to Susanoo. Takamimusubi is often depicted as a deity from before the separation of genders but is generally viewed as male. According to the closing section of the "Kamiyo" in the *Nihon Shoki*, Takamimusubi produced fifteen hundred offspring. Scholars like {{ill|Matsumae Takeshi|ja|松前健|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Takeshi|1=Takeshi Matsumae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11529297}} argue that the original form of Amaterasu was a male deity worshipped across Japan. == Notes == <references /> {{Reflist|qq=}} == References == * {{Citation|和書|author=Matsumae Takeshi|date=2007-12-30|title=日本神話の謎がよくわかる本|publisher=大和書房|ref={{SfnRef|松前健|2007|qq=}} |qq=}} == See also == * {{ill|Five Generations of Earth Deities|ja|地神五代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Generations of Earth Deities|qq=}} {{Shinto topics|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|皇祖神|version=99596967|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:皇祖神]]
<!--Ancestors of the Emperor-->
